VideoTitle: Sept. 18, 1998 football game vs. Thomas Sumter

Recap:
The Memorial Day School football team got a huge scare but was able to pull out a 20-16 win over St. Andrew's on Thursday night at Westside Stadium.

The Matadors kicked off to the Saints' Beau Hinton with only 21 seconds remaining in the game. Hinton returned the ball from his 5-yard line to his own 38. With 10.8 seconds showing on the clock, Saint quarterback Davey Clay threw the ball 50 yards and tight end Josh Smith hauled it in and raced to the 2-yard line before getting tackled.

Unfortunately for the Saints, they didn't have any more timeouts and the game ended before they could get another play off.

Memorial (2-0) running back Justin Black, who scored what turned out to be the game-winning touchdown - a 13-yard run with 2:28 left - breathed a sigh of relief after the final horn blew.

"That last play scared me," Black said. "I was getting ready to cry. We had a missed coverage on their tight end. I'm glad we're a fourth-quarter team, though."

Memorial first-year coach J.D. Garletts saw his team improve to 2-0. In the opener last week, Memorial rallied to defeat Edmund Burke 39-35.

"Thank goodness we got away with the win because St. Andrew's outplayed us," Garletts said. "We call ourselves a fourth-quarter team because we have a spectacular two-minute offense we can unleash anytime during the game."

For Garletts, a 1998 Savannah Country Day graduate, it has been a learning experience, especially since he walked into the job as head coach.

"I played football for four years at Hampden-Sydney College and when our football coach took another job, I was offered the job on the spot," Garletts said.

Clay, the Saints' senior leader, did everything in his power to keep his team in its 2003 opener. He and Smith almost pulled out a win for St. Andrew's.

"I was thinking that I had to get (to the goal line) because I knew we needed a touchdown," Smith said. "This is definitely a learning experience for us, and I have to give it to my teammates - we played hard."

The Saints (0-1) led 7-6 at halftime and took a 10-6 lead early in the third quarter when senior kicker Will Massey nailed a 40-yard field goal with room to spare.

St. Andrew's recovered two fumbles by the Matadors but could not capitalize on the opportunities.

After the game, a shoving match broke out between the schools' trainers after an argument. Coaches and Garden City police had to restore order.

ScoringPlays.Play: Mark Finger 33 pass from Charles Scarminach
Jacob Rivers 20 pass from Davy Clay

Recap:
Beau Hinton rushed for 133 yards and three touchdowns as St. Andrew's rolled past Hilton Head Prep 36-15 Friday night.

Hinton scored on runs of 1, 2 and 47 yards.

Davy Clay ran for a 2-yard touchdowns and threw a 20-yard pass to Jacob Rivers.

For Hilton Head, Mark Finger scored on a 33-yard pass from Charles Scarminach and Niles Murphy scored on a 99-yard kickoff return. St. Andrew's is 2-1; Hilton Head is 0-3.

ScoringPlays.Play: Dorian Capers 47 pass from Leon Etheridge

Recap:
RIDGELAND - Beau Hinton's second touchdown of the night, a 17-yard run, gave St. Andrew's the lead for good in the third quarter, and Davy Clay's 1-yard sneak sealed the SCISA non-region win.

Hinton's other score, a 13-yard score, tied the score at 6 in the second quarter. The Saints (3-1) then built a 19-6 lead before Thomas Heyward (2-4) capped the scoring on Leon Etheridge's 47-yard touchdown pass to Dorian Capers. Etheridge began the scoring with a 1-yard, second-quarter run for the Rebels, which outgained the Saints 286-188 but committed four turnovers.

Will Massey's 30-yard field goal in the third quarter was the lone score in St. Andrew's 3-0 victory over Calhoun.

Saints' Davey Clay preserved the win by falling on a Calhoun fumble in the end zone.

Beau Hinton led the winners with 102 yards on 25 carries. Omar Glenn added 96 yards on 11 carries.

St. Andrew's raised its record to 4-1 overall, 1-0 in region play.

DILLON, S.C. - Aaron Moody and Montrice Jones each had two touchdowns to lead Dillon Christian to a 32-7 victory over St. Andrew's.

Moody scored on runs of 1 and 6 yards. Jones had touchdown runs of 8 and 30 yards.

Davy Clay had the lone touchdown for the Saints (3-3) in the first quarter.

Dillon outgained St. Andrew's, 358-95.

HOLLY HILL, S.C. - St. Andrew's scored 34 points in the first half and coasted to a 41-0 victory over Holly Hill Academy Friday night in a SCISA 2-AA game.

Davy Clay got the Saints off to a fast start, scoring on a 3-yard run and then passing 15 yards to Josh Smith for a 14-0 lead.

Smith scored in the second quarter on a 34-yard interception return, Beau Hinton tallied on a 3-yard run and Will Massey scored on a 35-yard fumble recovery return to put SA up 34-0 at the half.

The only score in the second half came on Hinton's 19-yard run.

St. Andrew's is 5-3, 2-2 in region.

Holly Hill is 3-6, 0-4 in region.

ScoringPlays.Play: Josh Smith 2 pass from Davy Clay

Notes: The Game will be played at Bible Baptist School
